name: rad-planner
description:
**Plan a project before you write any code — or replan after a pivot, deepen an existing plan, or check for drift.** rad-planner walks you through a five-phase plan-first **conversation** with project-specific doc recommendations, then writes the approved doc set as the plan's first executed milestone. The plan is the deliverable; the docs follow the plan, not the other way around.

**v4.5 — `--assessment` flag exposed.** A read-only diagnostic pass that runs the internal assessor over whatever evidence exists (git, docs, source, validator results) and emits a structured report: project facts, canonical-doc coverage + staleness + validator pass/fail, detected drift signals, and an inferred next-step suggestion (full / improve / drift / pivot / `no planner action needed`) with one-sentence rationale. Distinct from `--drift` (which requires an existing plan to compare against): `--assessment` works on any project state and produces no writes. M0 discovery runs, then the assessor pass executes; M0.5 and M1–M5 are skipped. The right flag when the user asks "where does this project stand?" before choosing an entry point.

**v4.4 — project-story synthesis skills.** Two new skills derive plain-language narrative views from the canonical doc set: `/project-story` generates a state-of-the-project narrative (validated format: one-line product test, who it's for in build order, what it's explicitly NOT, where we are, what's pending, bottom line) for non-developer stakeholders, new collaborators, future-self after time away, or funders. `/refresh-story` updates an existing story file in place via inline drift detection — regenerates only the sections whose canonical source has changed, preserves the user's edits in fresh sections. Both skills read `docs/vision.md`, `docs/decisions/*.md`, `docs/planning/current.md`, `docs/status.md`, `docs/roadmap.md`. Rendering rules: plain English, no marketing language, every claim traceable to source.

**Conversational by design (v4.1) + scope-confirmation gate (v4.2) + briefing reshape & continuation triggers (v4.3).** `/plan` is multi-phase dialogue. The **M0.5 scope-confirmation hard gate** (v4.2) fires after M0 discovery and BEFORE any substantive work, on ALL entry points and ALL invocation modes including `--auto` — autonomy applies to the work, not to the scope decision. **v4.3 reshapes the M0.5 surface** from a terse identifier-dense recap into a prose-first briefing pattern matching rad-session's `/startup` doorman model — same hard gate, friendlier framing: **Where we left off** (plain prose, no schema names or bit numbers) + **Last accomplishment** + **Next logical step** + **Or you could start with** (2–3 concrete alternatives including a skip / execute / redirect option) + a single **Mechanical state** line for identifiers. Rendering rules forbid bullet lists of identifiers, ADR numbers, or schema column names in the friendly framing; those collapse into one Mechanical state line. **v4.3 also expands trigger phrases** to catch free-form planning-continuation utterances at session open — "continue the plan", "continue planning", "let's keep planning", "pick up the plan", "where did we leave off in the plan", "resume the plan", and similar — routing them to the improvement entry point so the M0.5 gate fires rather than letting them become ad-hoc agent work. M1–M5 each require explicit user input. Harness-level autonomous-mode signals (permission-mode Auto/Bypass, `<system-reminder>` hints about not asking clarifying questions) apply to tool-use approvals (bash, Write, Edit, git, MCP), NOT to the planning conversation. The only mode that bypasses M1–M5 dialogue is `--auto`, which produces DRAFT-labeled strawmen and **does NOT write ADRs** (candidate decisions land in `docs/planning/proposed-decisions.md` for user review). `--auto` still respects the M0.5 scope-confirmation gate. Default is always conversational.

`/plan` runs four entry points (full / improvement / drift / pivot) detected from utterance + project state, with a universal four-direction menu as the ambiguity fallback. Each entry routes through the five-phase conversation (or a subset):

- M1 Constitution & Frame — operating-manual content (Project, Read order, Hard boundaries, Engineering rules, Definition of done, Escalate triggers)
- M2 Goal-Backward Scope — must-be-TRUE / must-EXIST / CRITICAL vs nice-to-have, hardest unknown, derailment risks, non-goals (GSD-flavored scaffold)
- M3 Sequence with Size Discipline — milestones with 2-3 tasks each, ~50% context bar, dependencies mapped, risk-first ordering
- M4 Quality Gates — per-milestone acceptance criteria, validation commands, stop conditions, notes-for-next-session, project-wide Definition of Done
- M5 Doc-Set Recommendation — complexity routing (lite / standard / full), project-specific rationale per doc, user_approval hard gate before any writes

M6 executes the plan's first milestone: writes the approved doc set per the canonical structure — operating manual (CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md per agent scope), `docs/vision.md`, `docs/architecture.md`, `docs/planning/current.md`, `docs/status.md` scaffold (rad-session populates from evidence), `docs/decisions/` (default mode only; `--auto` writes `docs/planning/proposed-decisions.md` instead), optional `docs/roadmap.md`, `.rad/profile`. Guard rails protect existing user content — overwrite/append/skip prompts for pre-existing strategic docs, external init residue enriched not overwritten, code never touched.

Mode flags: `--full` (greenfield), `--improve` (deepen or continue existing plan), `--drift` (assessment against existing plan), `--pivot` (substantial re-plan with disposition manifest), `--validate` (utility — run validators only), `--assessment` (NEW in 4.5: read-only state-of-project diagnostic, no plan required, no writes), `--auto` (4.1: unattended DRAFT strawman, no ADRs). Plus `--agents claude_only|codex_only|claude_and_codex`, `--non-interactive` (CI/scripted, distinct from `--auto`), `--resume <run-id>`, `--output-dir <path>`.

Four mechanical validators back the conversational workflow:
- `plan-lint.py` — required-section presence, AC checkbox format, vague-language detection on `planning/current.md`
- `status-validator.py` — freshness vs git mtime, 8-section presence, evidence-based validation results on `status.md`
- `doc-redundancy.py` — cross-doc bullet duplicate detection via Jaccard similarity
- `doc-contradiction.py` — vision.md non-goals vs current.md acceptance criteria via stemmed token overlap

Plus `validate-json.py` for subagent JSON contracts (unchanged from 3.0). Two test fixtures under `fixtures/` exercise the validators end-to-end.

Always-teaches in `/plan` — every phase explains why it asks what it asks; every doc recommendation includes project-specific rationale. Mentor vs dev mode (project-scoped in `.rad/profile`) gates teaching depth at `/wrapup` (via rad-session) but not at `/plan`.

Aligned with the published OpenAI/Anthropic project-structure research: three-band project memory model (operating manual / project memory / execution state), intent-vs-reality split between `planning/current.md` (plan) and `status.md` (evidence), AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md as conditional operating manuals based on agent scope. Canonical spec at [`docs/doc-conventions.md`](https://github.com/radesjardins/RAD-Claude-Skills/blob/main/docs/doc-conventions.md).

Pairs naturally with rad-session for the full project lifecycle, but works standalone. Migration tooling from 3.0 not included — neither prior version had public traffic.
